response.justification: Wei Chen is a strong individual contributor with 11+ years at senior/staff level and excellent distributed-systems expertise, but the CTO role requires executive leadership capabilities that are entirely absent from the resume. There is no evidence of team scaling, people management, or organizational development—the career shows a pure IC trajectory. Additionally, fintech infrastructure depth is minimal (only RIDE Capital, ~1 year of loose exposure), and multi-agent AI production experience is extremely recent (<1 year at re:cinq) with unclear scope beyond contributing to MCP tooling. The candidate would be an excellent Principal Engineer hire but lacks the foundational leadership and domain prerequisites for a CTO role.
strengths: Deep distributed-systems expertise from high-throughput backend work at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, real-time metadata, low-latency apps)
11+ years at senior/staff level with demonstrated adaptability across multiple technology stacks and architectures (Node.js, DLang, C++, event-driven platforms)
Event-driven architecture and real-time systems experience, including open-source GISCollective platform and contributions to AI-agent infrastructure tooling (MCP)
concerns: No evidence of leading or scaling engineering teams—entire career is individual-contributor roles with no mention of hiring, mentoring, or organizational development

Minimal fintech infrastructure depth; only RIDE Capital (1 year) shows tangential investment-domain exposure; no credit systems, payment flows, working-capital, or compliance infrastructure experience

Multi-agent AI production experience is extremely recent (<1 year at re:cinq) and limited to contributing to developer tooling/frameworks rather than designing and operating agent systems as core business infrastructure

key_factors.factor: Proven multi-agent AI architecture at scale
